observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the 

(a) Property Officer to immediately conduct actual count of inventories and forward the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books; 

(b) Municipal Accountant to 

(i) prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards to record the acquisition, issuance and disposal of inventories; and 

(ii) to record all acquisitions of inventories to the appropriate Inventory account and recognize the equivalent expense account upon receipt of Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) from the Property Unit; and 

(c) Property Officer to 

(i) use the RIS in the issuance of inventories to end-users, and summarize the RIS monthly through the RSMI; and 

(ii) submit a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported with Acknowledgement Receipts of the donations in-kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, not necessarily in the required form; and Inventory of the remaining undistributed items, if any.

We recommended that the LCE 

(a) activate and strengthen the Gender and Development Focal Point System (GFPS) to ensure the mainstreaming of Gender and Development (GAD) in the systems and processes of the Municipality; 

(b) require the GFPS to exert efforts to come up with a database on sex-disaggregated data or perform gender analysis to ensure that allocation of funds on GAD-related programs, projects and activities (PPAs) are not based on incorrect assumptions and stereotypes; and 

(c) coordinate with the SB to expedite the enactment of the Municipality’s GAD Code.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the (a) MA to revert to the CIB-LCCA account in the accounting books the unreleased/cancelled/stale checks accordingly; (b) MT to (i) update the recording of the checks issued in  the Check Register at year-end; and (ii) report in  the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) the checks actually released to payees which shall be the basis of the MA in the recording of disbursements in the Check Disbursements Journal; (c) MT and MA to (i) continuously comply with the guidelines on reporting and accounting of collections and deposits in accordance with the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and (ii) conduct regular reconciliation of the cash records to immediately address the deficiencies thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the MA to consistently apply the PIM in the recording of inventories upon purchase; (b) require the MA and Supply Officer to prepare and maintain SLCs and SCs, respectively, for control and   monitoring of inventories; and (c) require the Supply Officer to conduct physical count of inventories and submit the results thereof to the MAO and Audit Team for reference in audit.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the completion of the physical count of PPEs by referring to COA Circular No. 2020-006 to take advantage of the one-time clean-up of the PPE account that will also facilitate the preparation of PPELC and PCs; (b) instruct the designated official to prepare the IIRUP to account for all unserviceable PPEs and dispose them in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations; and (c) require  the designated OIC-GSO to  perform his property custodial and accountability functions according to the duties and responsibilities of the office.

We recommended and the LCE agreed (a) that in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), Municipal Budget Officer (MBO), and ME to fully utilize and implement the identified PPAs under the 20% DF to attain the objective of providing timely socio-economic benefits to its constituents; and (b) to require the ME to (i) comply with the guidelines in the preparation/submission of ABC; and (ii) support  the disbursements on completed projects  with  the construction logbook and  progress photos.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) ME/BAC Chairman to comply with the documentary requirements on alternative modes of procurement; and (b) MA in coordination with the ME to submit the lacking documentary requirements in the noted transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) ME/BAC Chairperson to require from the prospective bidders the submission of procurement documents consisting of the PhilGEPS Registration Certificate, related PERT-CPM Diagram, BOQ, and plans/drawings; and (b) MA to require the submission of the lacking documentary requirements noted on the project payments.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA, in coordination with other concerned Municipal Offices to (a) exert efforts in identifying the source of funds for the unused balances amounting to ₱2,571,384.21 and ₱3,124,006.94 lodged to the Due to NGAs and Due to Other LGUs accounts, respectively; (b) otherwise, revert the unexpended balance of the transferred funds to the National Treasury; and (c) prepare subsidiary ledgers for fund transfers and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of fund transfers to the source agencies.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MPDC and MA to (a) implement only PPAs that are incorporated in the LDRRMP and integrated in the approved AIP of the Municipality; (b) prepare the quarterly reports on fund utilization and status of program/project implementation; and (c) submit the proof of posting of the quarterly reports to the Municipality’s website, and in at least three conspicuous places and other reporting requirements as required under DBM LBC Nos. 130, 134, and 136.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (i) set-up a Trust Liability account for the unspent balance of the LDRRMF; (ii) submit on a monthly basis the report on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF based on the required format of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and (iii) ensure that supporting documents are complete and in order before payment to contractors and suppliers are  made.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) BAC to (i) post the notice of award, contract or purchase order, including notice to proceed in the PhilGEPS website and at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in all cases of alternative mode of procurement; and (b) MA, (i) in coordination with the BAC, to submit the documentary requirements on the emergency procurement such as Mayor’s Permit and Omnibus Sworn Statement; and (ii) to recognize as Assets the Medical and Supplies upon purchase and as Expense upon issuance to end-users pursuant to Section 144 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MA to workback on the differences noted and ensure that taxes withheld are remitted in full within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) instruct the HRMO to (i) update the information of employees submitted to the GSIS; and (ii) ensure that the contributions deducted from each employee are reconciled with GSIS records; and (b) require the MA to (i) check if the arrears paid were actually withheld; and (ii)make the corresponding adjustments in the books of accounts if warranted.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) instruct the HRMO (i) to check the employee list uploaded in the EPRS to ensure that the list is correct and up to date; and (ii) counter check the amounts of premiums due per employee on the generated SPA vis-à-vis the withheld per payroll; and (b) require the MA to check the amounts of premiums withheld and remitted in the books to ensure correctness of data and avoid discrepancies.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the HRMO and MAO to check the detailed list provided by Pag-IBIG vis-à-vis the payroll to ensure correct premiums deductions and ensure that remittances are made in full.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the GAD Focal Point System to (a) exert efforts to come up with a database on sex-disaggregated data or perform gender analysis to ensure that allocation of funds on GAD-related PPAs are not based on incorrect assumptions and stereotypes; (b) initiate the capacitation of key members of the GFPS on the use of HGDG tool to guarantee the integration of gender concerns in the design, management, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of the agency programs and projects and to determine the efficiency and effectiveness of the LGU’s mainstreaming efforts; and (c) create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team  to evaluate the outcomes of the GAD PPAs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer to 

(i) update the Treasury Office records by recording all the identified cash transactions that were not recorded and adjusted in the cashbooks for the cash in bank account; and 

(ii) to regularly review and verify the entries in the cashbooks to ensure the correctness of the Cashbook balances at the end of each month; and 

(b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct quarterly reconciliation of their records for immediate correction/adjustment of the discrepancies.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other concerned Municipal officials to 

(a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports for the timely verification and audit of accounts; 

(b) observe the prescribed dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team; and 

(c) submit all DVs for the period January to December 2020 within 15 days following the issuance of the Audit Observation Memorandum to preclude the suspension of transactions.

We recommended that the Local Development Council chaired by the LCE 

(a) address the issue on generic PPAs by including the brief description and the details of the PPAs in the AIP for the ensuing year; and 

(b) utilize the 20% DF only for desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes which are essential to the attainment of the ultimate objective of improving the quality of life for the Municipality’s constituents and the public.

We recommended that the LCE require compliance with the provisions of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 by directing the Bids and Awards Committee/BAC Secretariat to require from prospective bidders the submission of (i) statement of all its ongoing government and private contracts, including contracts awarded but not yet started, if any, whether similar or not similar in nature and complexity to the contract to be bid; (ii) statement of SLCC which is similar in nature to the project; and (iii) list of equipment owned or leased pledged to the contract complete with details such as model/year, capacity/performance/size, plate number, motor/body number, and number of units owned as well as the proof of ownership; (iv) financial statements in conformity with the requirements of RA No. 9184; and (v) COSHP for the  infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to require all winning bidders to cause the extension of their performance security to cover approved contract time extensions and remain valid until issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance pursuant to Section 39.6 of the IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the LCE require the 

(a) Municipal Engineer to 

(i) send a written communication to the Contractors by requiring them to provide notices requesting extension of contract time and citing therein the reasons for the delay in project completion; 

(ii) examine the facts and extent of the delay and recommend to the LCE the approval or disapproval of the Contract Time Extension; and 

(iii) if warranted, account for the negative slippage and impose liquidated damages to the Contractor; and

(b) Municipal Accountant to account for the negative slippage and compute for the liquidated damages to be charged to the Contractor for failure to satisfactorily complete the work within the specified contract time.

We recommended that the LCE direct the 

(a) Municipal Engineer to 

(i) submit the deficient contract documents such as the ABC, POW, plans/drawings and technical specifications; 

(ii) comply with the guidelines on information and publicity on programs, projects and activities of government agencies; 

(iii) complete the project activity logbook to indicate all required information such as the status of the on-going activity, new works started, construction equipment at the site each day and if operational or idle, the number of supervisory and non-supervisory personnel on site, and visitors to the site; and 

(iv) submit the revised PERT-CPM of the completed project “Construction of Evacuation Center at San Marcos”; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant to 

(i) require the submission of mandatory supporting documents common to all infrastructure projects pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and the minimum materials testing requirements for civil works pursuant to DPWH DO No. 11 series of 2017 dated January 24, 2017 to support the payments of the completed projects; and 

(ii) ensure that the release of the retention money is substituted with irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand, of amounts equivalent to the retention money substituted for and acceptable to Government.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC, Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to 

(a)  submit the documentary requirements on the emergency procurement made from various suppliers; 

(b) adopt competitive public bidding in the procurement of goods and services; and 

(c) submit to the COA Auditor the 33 unsubmitted DVs together with the required supporting documents to preclude the issuance of a notice of suspension.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to 

(i) settle government payables/obligations by check unless when it is impractical to do so, hence in such cases, payments may be made in the form of cash through cash advance in accordance with COA rules and regulations; and 

(ii) grant cash advances to authorized SDOs in accordance with the conditions provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to withhold the correct taxes for every transaction and remit the same to the BIR within the prescribed period to avoid fines, penalties and other sanctions for non-compliance with rules and regulations set forth by the BIR.
We recommended that the LCE require the concerned implementing offices to continue being compliant to the rules and regulations on the use of government funds and posting requirements.

We recommended that the LCE 

(a) submit the Office Orders of Municipal officials and personnel required to work during the ECQ/MECQ for the entitlement of hazard pay; and 

(b) require department heads to submit the schedule of employees required to work during the pandemic and the dates and purpose of their work assignments; certificate of attendance during the ECQ/MECQ period/ Daily Time Record (DTR); and certificate of service rendered or accomplishment report during the ECQ/MECQ.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO), to furnish the Audit Team the MOA, liquidation report and payrolls for the first and second tranche for verification and audit.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to require all concerned Municipal officials to submit the lacking documentary requirements and henceforth, ensure that transactions of the Municipality would comply with existing laws and regulations to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee to 

(a) submit a written explanation/justification on the noted observations/deviations; 

(b) submit the bidding documents and supporting documents for progress and final billing immediately to preclude the noted transactions from being suspended or disallowed in audit; and 

(c) henceforth, ensure that the necessary supporting documents attached to disbursement vouchers are complete and in order prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE

(a) coordinate with the SB to expedite the enactment of the Municipality’s GAD Code with assurance that all its essential elements are contained therein pursuant to RA No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 which amended PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No 2013-01: Guidelines on the Localization on the Magna Carta of Women

(b) reconstitute the GFPS; and 

(c) require the GFPS to ensure that only GAD related expenditures that will address identified eligible gender issues are implemented and charged under GAD Funds.

1.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a)
Municipal Treasurer to (i) update the Treasury Office records by recording all the
identified cash transactions that were not recorded and adjusted in the cashbooks for
the cash in bank account; and (ii) to regularly review and verify the entries in the
cashbooks to ensure the correctness of the Cashbook balances at the end of each
month; and (b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct quarterly
reconciliation of their records for immediate correction/adjustment of the
discrepancies.

2.19. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Property Officer to
immediately conduct actual count of inventories and forward the Report of Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books;
(b) Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards to
record the acquisition, issuance and disposal of inventories; and (ii) to record all
acquisitions of inventories to the appropriate Inventory account and recognize the
equivalent expense account upon receipt of Report of Supplies and Materials Issued
(RSMI) supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) from the Property Unit; and
(c) Property Officer to (i) use the RIS in the issuance of inventories to end-users, and
summarize the RIS monthly through the RSMI; and (ii) submit a Summary/List of
Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported with Acknowledgement
Receipts of the donations in-kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to the
beneficiaries, not necessarily in the required form; and Inventory of the remaining
undistributed items, if any.

3.20. We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer as General Services Officer to (i) maintain property ledger
cards and PPE ledger cards to substantiate the existence, completeness and reliability
of items composing the respective PPE accounts; (ii) facilitate the reconciliation
process for identification of any discrepancies in PPE records; and (iii) work on the
completion of physical inventory taking and prepare the Report of Physical Count of
PPE; (b) designate a property officer to discharge the property management custodial
functions of the Municipality; (c) require the Municipal Accountant to submit all the
unsubmitted disbursement vouchers with transaction value of ₱2,404,156.74 for the
payment to contractors and suppliers for the procured PPE and infrastructure
projects; (d) submit the lacking documentary requirements to support the payment
on the procured parcel of land costing ₱4,685,448.00; and (e) submit justification on
the necessity of the procurement of 13 units of Apple iPads to preclude the transaction
from being disallowed in audit.

4.9. We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant, in
coordination with other concerned Municipal officials to (a) strictly comply with the
regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports for the timely
verification and audit of accounts; (b) observe the prescribed dates of submission of
the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team; and (c) submit all
DVs for the period January to December 2020 within 15 days following the issuance
of the Audit Observation Memorandum to preclude the suspension of transactions.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) trace and check the accounting entries made in connection with the loans payable account; and 

(b) make the necessary correcting entries, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to

(a) reclassify the accounts incorrectly lodged to Due to LGUs account amounting to ₱4,005,872.36 to their appropriate accounts; and 

(b) prepare and keep a separate subsidiary ledger for each fund transfer to be able to track information at a very detailed level at any given time.

We recommended that the LCE 

(a) require the concerned Municipal officials to 

(i) expedite the full implementation of the identified development projects under the Funds received from DOLE totaling ₱300,000.00 and Assistance to Municipalities from DILG totaling ₱12,709,000.00 for optimum attainment of the intent of the fund; 

(ii) exert efforts in identifying the source of funds amounting to ₱957,541.65 for the delivery of project to the intended beneficiaries without further delay; 

(iii) prepare project proposal for the use of the ₱100,000.00 and unexpended balance of ₱2,641,784.21 and seek approval from the source agencies; and 

(b) require the Municipal Accountant to 

(i) prepare quarterly report of the unexpended balances of fund transfers and reconcile with the source agencies the unexpended balances; and 

(ii) strictly adhere with the above-mentioned rules and regulation in the maintenance of trust funds.

We recommended that the LCE
(a) instruct the Municipal Development Council to 

(i) perform due diligence in the identification and prioritization of the development projects; and 

(ii) include only PPAs that are eligible for funding under the 20% DF; and 

(b) submit the Local Sanggunian Resolution authorizing the implementation of programs or projects described in generic terms in the AIP; and 

(c) require the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to submit the necessary documents to support the payments of the completed projects to avoid suspension of payments made.

We recommended that the LCE require the 

(a) Municipal Accountant to 

(i) submit accurate monthly reports on the sources and utilization of MDRRMF based on the required format of COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; 

(ii) refer to the same COA Circulars for the accounting entries to recognize transactions related to DRRMF; 

(iii) facilitate the immediate transfer of the remaining balance of the 5% DRMMF to the special trust fund account; and 

(iv) set-up a Trust Liability account for the unspent balance of the MDRRMF; and 

(b) HRMO to create the required position Local DRRM Officer III and ensure that the qualifications required by the CSC for the position are met by the designated Head of Office.

We recommended that the LCE 

(a) reconstitute the Municipal SWMB; and

(b) require the MENRO to strictly enforce and monitor the use of plastic bags within the locality and firmly impose fines and penalties against violators of prohibited acts; and 

(c) through the Sangguniang Bayan, enact an ordinance prohibiting the littering and dumping of waste materials in public places.

We recommended that the LCE

(a) coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the rationalization and revision of the LRC to generate more revenues for the local government; 

(b) identify the problems that face real property tax collection of the Municipality and enforce the procedures and remedies in addressing tax delinquencies as provided by Section 174 of RA No. 7160; and 

(c) require the Municipal Market Administrator to 

(i) regulate the public and private stalls in the public market in accordance with the Market Code for an effective facilitation of activities within the market; and 

(ii) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer on matters of collection and imposition of fines and charges.

We recommended that the LCE

(a) require the HRMO to 

(i) refer to the specific provisions of CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017 and CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No 1, Series of 2018 for policy guidelines on hiring of JO, casuals, and consultants; 

(ii) facilitate the submission of verified DTRs and accomplishment reports to substantiate payment of salaries and allowances of JOs and casual personnel; 

(iii) revise the contract of service of JOs to include the duties and responsibilities and provision that will state that the existing qualified JOs and casuals may be considered for appointment to vacant positions subject to existing rules of the CSC; and 

(iv) proceed with the management commitment of utilizing the time and attendance system to obtain accurate attendance for reporting and payroll processing; and 

(b) require the Municipal Accountant to review the payroll at period-end and ensure that DTRs and accomplishment reports are attached thereto to support claims for payment.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) explain the unresolved discrepancies between the audited and reported balance of the Due to BIR account; and henceforth 

(b) make the necessary adjustments to revert unreleased checks pertaining to unremitted taxes to the Due to BIR account at year-end to ensure accuracy of the reported balance of the account; and 

(c) implement the correct computation of withholding taxes from suppliers/contractors.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant deduct/withhold income taxes on payment of honoraria of the BAC members, BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group, and remit the same to the BIR on the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant 

(a) to hereafter make the necessary adjustments to revert unreleased checks pertaining to unremitted contributions to the Due to Pag-IBIG account at year-end to ensure accuracy of the reported balance of the account; 

(b) provide a written explanation on the unresolved discrepancies between the amounts in the source documents and the GL and settle the discrepancies noted; and 

(c) closely monitor the actual contributions withheld and remitted vis-a-vis the amounts recorded in the GL.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant 

(a) to hereafter make the necessary adjustments to revert unreleased checks pertaining to unremitted contributions to the Due to GSIS account at yearend to ensure accuracy of the reported balance of the account; 

(b) provide a written explanation on the unresolved discrepancies between the amounts in the source documents and the GL and settle the discrepancies noted; and 

(c) closely monitor the actual contributions withheld and remitted vis-a-vis the amounts recorded in the GL.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant 

(a) to hereafter make the necessary adjustments to revert unreleased checks pertaining to unremitted contributions to the Due to PhilHealth account at yearend to ensure accuracy of the reported balance of the account; 

(b) provide a written explanation on the unresolved discrepancies between the amounts in the source documents and the GL and settle the discrepancies noted; and 

(c) closely monitor the actual contributions withheld and remitted vis-a-vis the amounts recorded in the GL.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the officials concerned to:

(a) exert their best efforts to retrieve the documents necessary for the titling of the parcels of land under the Municipality’s name; 

(b) speed up the completion of inventory taking and submit the RPCPPE; and 

(c) establish and maintain updated PPELCs and PCs to facilitate reconciliation of accounting and property records.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to implement the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) require the MAO (i) to make the necessary accounting entries to book up the unrecognized PPE in the books; (ii) review the Trust Fund books to identify the purchased PPE in previous years that were not recognized in the books; (iii) be assertive in obtaining information related to donated/transferred PPE to the Municipality to ensure their timely recognition in the books; and (iv) provide depreciation for all depreciable assets; and (b) fully utilize the GSO unit by consolidating all property management related tasks under its turf including but not limited to the (i) exertion of efforts to gather the necessary documents for titling of lots under the name of the Municipality; (ii) establishment and maintenance of property cards for each class of PPE; (iii) completion of physical inventory taking; (iv) preparation of the Report of Physical Count of PPE; (v) safekeeping and custody of property documents; and (vi) promulgation of policies to achieve sufficient sound property management practices within the agency.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to trace and check the accounting entries made in connection with the loans payable account and make the necessary correcting entries, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to (a) reclassify the accounts incorrectly lodged to Due to LGUs account amounting to ₱4,005,872.36 to their appropriate accounts; and (b) prepare and keep a separate subsidiary ledger for each fund transfer to be able to track information at a very detailed level at any given time.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the concerned Municipal officials to (i) expedite the full implementation of the identified development projects under the Funds received from DOLE  totaling ₱300,000.00 and Assistance to Municipalities from DILG totaling ₱12,709,000.00 for optimum attainment of the intent of the fund; (ii) exert efforts in identifying the source of funds amounting to ₱957,541.65 for the delivery of project to the intended beneficiaries without further delay; (c) prepare project proposal for the use of the ₱100,000.00 and unexpended balance of ₱2,641,784.21 and seek approval from the source agencies; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare quarterly report of the unexpended balances of fund transfers and reconcile with the source agencies the unexpended balances; and (ii) strictly adhere with the above-mentioned rules and regulation in the maintenance of trust funds.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee to (a) submit a written explanation/justification on the noted observations/deviations; (b) submit the bidding documents and supporting documents for progress and final billing immediately to preclude the noted transactions from being suspended or disallowed in audit; and (c) henceforth, ensure that the necessary supporting documents attached to disbursement vouchers are complete and in order prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the Municipal Development Council to (i) perform due diligence in the identification and prioritization of the development projects; and (ii) include only PPAs that are eligible for funding under the 20% DF; and (b) submit the Local Sanggunian Resolution authorizing the implementation of programs or projects described in generic terms in the AIP; (c) require the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to submit the necessary documents to support the payments of the completed projects to avoid suspension of payments made.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) submit accurate monthly reports on the sources and utilization of MDRRMF based on the required format of COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; (ii) refer to the same COA Circulars for the accounting entries to recognize transactions related to DRRMF; (iii) facilitate the immediate transfer of the remaining balance of the 5% DRMMF to the special trust fund account; and (iv) set-up a Trust Liability account for the unspent balance of the MDRRMF; and (b) HRMO to create the required position Local DRRM Officer III and ensure that the qualifications required by the CSC for the position are met by the designated Head of Office.

We recommended that the LCE (a) reconstitute the Municipal SWMB; (b) require the MENRO to strictly enforce and monitor the use of plastic bags within the locality and firmly impose fines and penalties against violators of prohibited acts; and (c) through the Sangguniang Bayan, enact an ordinance prohibiting the littering and dumping of waste materials in public places.

We recommended that the LCE (a) coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the rationalization and revision of the LRC to generate more revenues for the local government; (b) identify the problems that face real property tax collection of the Municipality and enforce the procedures and remedies in addressing tax delinquencies as provided by Section 174 of RA No. 7160; and (c) require the Municipal Market Administrator to (i) regulate the public and private stalls in the public market in accordance with the Market Code for an effective facilitation of activities within the market; and (ii) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer on matters of collection and imposition of fines and charges.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the HRMO to (i) refer to the specific provisions of CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017 and CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No 1, Series of 2018 for policy guidelines on hiring of JO, casuals, and consultants; (ii) facilitate the submission of verified DTRs and accomplishment reports to substantiate payment of salaries and allowances of JOs and casual personnel; (iii) revise the contract of service of JOs to include the duties and responsibilities and provision that will state that the existing qualified JOs and casuals may be considered for appointment to vacant positions subject to existing rules of the CSC; and (iv) proceed with the management commitment of utilizing the time and attendance system to obtain accurate attendance for reporting and payroll processing; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to review the payroll at period-end and ensure that DTRs and accomplishment reports are attached thereto to support claims for payment.

We recommended that the LCE (a) coordinate with the SB to expedite the enactment of the Municipality’s  GAD Code with assurance that all its essential elements are contained therein pursuant to RA No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 which amended PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No 2013-01: Guidelines on the Localization on the Magna Carta of Women (b) reconstitute the GFPS; and (c) require the GFPS to ensure that only GAD related expenditures that will address identified eligible gender issues are implemented and charged under GAD Funds.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) explain the unresolved discrepancies between the audited and reported balance of the Due to BIR account; and henceforth (b) make the necessary adjustments to revert unreleased checks pertaining to unremitted taxes to the Due to BIR account at year-end to ensure accuracy of the reported balance of the account; and (c) implement the correct computation of withholding taxes from suppliers/contractors.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant deduct/withhold income taxes on payment of honoraria of the BAC members, BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group, and remit the same to the BIR on the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant (a) to hereafter make the necessary adjustments to revert unreleased checks pertaining to unremitted contributions to the Due to Pag-IBIG account at year-end to ensure accuracy of the reported balance of the account; (b) provide a written explanation on the unresolved discrepancies between the amounts in the source documents and the GL and settle the discrepancies noted; and (c) closely monitor the actual contributions withheld and remitted vis-a-vis the amounts recorded in the GL.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant (a) to hereafter make the necessary adjustments to revert unreleased checks pertaining to unremitted contributions to the Due to GSIS account at yearend to ensure accuracy of the reported balance of the account; (b) provide a written explanation on the unresolved discrepancies between the amounts in the source documents and the GL and settle the discrepancies noted; and (c) closely monitor the actual contributions withheld and remitted vis-a-vis the amounts recorded in the GL.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant (a) to hereafter make the necessary adjustments to revert unreleased checks pertaining to unremitted contributions to the Due to PhilHealth account at yearend to ensure accuracy of the reported balance of the account; (b) provide a written explanation on the unresolved discrepancies between the amounts in the source documents and the GL and settle the discrepancies noted; and (c) closely monitor the actual contributions withheld and remitted vis-a-vis the amounts recorded in the GL.
We recommended that the LCE prioritize the 20% DF on eligible projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) continue being compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities, and aim further in providing its MDRRMC personnel with advanced training on disaster preparedness and rescue operations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) through the MENRO 

(a) effect the closure of the open dumpsite and its immediate rehabilitation; and 

(b) monitor the strict compliance on the enacted Ordinances on the (i) use and sale of plastic and polyethylene as bagging and packing materials; (ii) no littering policy; and (iii) segregation of wastes at source.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to implement the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to comply with regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

observations.managementAction:
Management committed to comply with the recommendations. According to the Municipal Assessor, one lot is already supported with a Deed of Sale, and the titling thereof is in process. 

The completion of inventory taking is ongoing and the maintenance of PPELCs and PCs is being undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to invest in high–impact or developmental projects and include in the Annual Investment Plan only eligible projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended that Management continue to being compliant with the laws and regulations on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities by aiming further to provide MDRRMC personnel with advanced training on disaster preparedness and rescue operations.
observations.managementAction: Management  assured  that it will continue to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the MENRO (a) make provisions for the closure of the open dumpsite; and (b) monitor  the strict compliance on the mandatory segregation of wastes at source, use and sale of plastic and polyethylene as bagging and packing materials and the no littering policy.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that it will prepare a dialogue with the Sangguniang Bayan Chairman on Environment to make a separate ordinance on the segregation policy and on “No Burning of Wastes” as mandated in the Clean Air Act and to fully comply with the provisions of RA 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the General Services Office/BAC Secretariat to procure its common-use supplies requirements through the Procurement Service of the DBM effective immediately and consider them in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan for the succeeding years.

We recommended that Management comply strictly with the prescribed period of five working days for the submission of purchase orders and contracts along with the supporting documents, and within twenty four hours for notices of deliveries, pursuant to the aforementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with our audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with our audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office and Treasury Office to invariably comply with the full remittance of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th day of the following month in compliance with Section 6 of RA No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with our audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) continue to provide adequate budget for its GAD Plan; (b) include programs in the GAD Plan that directly benefit the women sector, as encouraged in the Philippine Plan for Gender and Development 1995-2025; and (c) exert more effort to comply with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-NEDA and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction:
Management agreed to comply with our audit recommendations.

Management will continue its efforts towards effective planning, implementation and monitoring of its GAD Program. It will exert more effort to comply with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-NEDA and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government should only make payments of such nature when adequately supported by Government Official Receipts.
observations.managementAction: Required government official receipts and stopped payment of annual dues of officers and employees to their respective associations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Treasury Office to exercise its lawful functions and responsibilities in collecting all taxes, fees, and charges of the LGU; (b) provide a safety vault/box in the Market Office; (c) stop designating Job Order personnel as collecting officers since they are not considered employees of the Municipality; and (d) effect the hiring of Revenue Collection Clerks who will discharge the collection functions.
observations.managementAction: Purchased  safety vault.
observations.reason: Due to budget constraints, we have not hired Revenue Collection Clerks yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board and the Municipal Government utilize the SEF in consonance with the laws and regulations prescribing the activities for the priority spending to attain its objectives and to take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprogramming, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Revised the annual budget to conform with the guidelines under Sec. 1 of RA 5447, DEEP, DBM and DILG Joint Circular no. 01-A

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to invest in high–impact or developmental projects and include in the Annual Investment Plan only eligible projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended that Management (a) continue to improve its compliance on the mandatory segregation of wastes at source, no segregation/no collection rule, and on having functional Materials Recovery Facility in its barangay; (b) fully implement the no littering ordinance; and (c) address the issue on the controlled open dumpsite and design the sanitary landfills in accordance with the guidelines set by the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
observations.reason: A memorandum of agreement between the Municipal Government of Calumpit, Bulacan and Metro Clark Waste Management Corporation was made on January 17, 2017 for disposal of residual solid waste.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to being compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities and aim further to providing its MDRRMC personnel with advanced training on disaster preparedness and rescue operations.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office and Treasury Office to invariably comply with the full remittance of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th day of the following month in compliance with Section 6 of RA No. 8291.
